title: UI copy clarity
summary: Keep UI headings, labels, helper text, and CTAs distinct enough that users can tell what each element means and which action to take.
decision: Whether headings, labels, helper text, and CTA copy are distinct enough for a first-time user to understand hierarchy and next action.

intent.purpose: Prevent generated interfaces from sounding internally consistent while still making distinct controls and hierarchy hard to understand.
intent.failure_mode: Generated UI repeats or semantically overlaps copy across headings, support text, and controls until different elements sound interchangeable and the next action becomes ambiguous.
intent.good_judgment: Give each UI copy element one clear role, keep adjacent actions semantically distinct, and use helper text to clarify rather than restate the label beside it.
acceptable_variation: reuse approved product terminology when the element role stays clear
shorten labels for surface constraints without collapsing distinct actions
adapt helper text by device or density while keeping the same intent
align with design-system naming conventions when they preserve action clarity
non_negotiables: no semantically overlapping action labels for different controls in the same decision cluster
no helper text that merely repeats the adjacent CTA or heading
no repeated section, control, and support copy that obscures primary versus secondary emphasis
no copy reuse that makes different actions or destinations sound interchangeable

detection.decision_question: Do the headings, labels, helper text, and CTA copy each have a distinct role, or does repeated language make the interface harder to understand or act on?
signals: repeated CTA nouns or verbs in one cluster
near-duplicate labels for different UI elements
helper text restating the CTA instead of clarifying it
section labels, headlines, and button text collapsing into the same phrase
copy reuse that hides primary versus secondary emphasis

thresholds.pass: each visible copy element has a distinct role and the next action is easy to identify
thresholds.warn: copy overlap is present but can be fixed by rewriting labels or support text without changing information architecture
thresholds.fail: different UI elements or actions are effectively synonymous and the next action becomes ambiguous

low.description: Rewrite the overlapping copy into clearer, role-specific labels and support text.

medium.description: Rewrite the copy and route the result through design review for clarity confirmation.

high.description: Block the output and require design review because the interface actions are no longer clearly distinguishable.
